title: carlina officinalis
intro: Carlina officinalis, also known as Carline Thistle or Holy Thistle, is a biennial plant in the sunflower family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the digestive system and promote the healing of wounds. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including indigestion, bloating, and menstrual cramps, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, Carlina officinalis is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal benefits, and it is also used as a food source for bees, which are drawn to its purple flowers.
